facts.text: Johannes Gutenberg is widely associated with the movable-type printing press.
His work in Mainz helped make books far cheaper and more widely available in Europe.
The Gutenberg Bible became one of the most famous early printed books.
Histories of printing and publishing almost always highlight Johannes Gutenberg when discussing the movable-type printing press.
Johannes Gutenberg's work helped move printing and publishing forward in practical, everyday ways.
Modern uses of technology related to the movable-type printing press still reflect the legacy of Johannes Gutenberg.
Science and technology museums often feature Johannes Gutenberg when explaining the history of printing and publishing.
Students of invention often encounter Johannes Gutenberg as a key figure in the story of the movable-type printing press.
The story of the movable-type printing press is one of the reasons Johannes Gutenberg remains so well known today.
Johannes Gutenberg is remembered as one of the major names connected to the movable-type printing press and printing and publishing.
Benjamin Franklin is widely associated with the lightning rod, bifocals, and the Franklin stove.
Franklin's electrical experiments made him one of the most famous scientific figures of the 18th century.
He preferred not to patent many of his ideas because he believed useful discoveries should benefit the public.
Histories of practical science and everyday technology almost always highlight Benjamin Franklin when discussing the lightning rod, bifocals, and the Franklin stove.
Benjamin Franklin's work helped move practical science and everyday technology forward in practical, everyday ways.

Benjamin Franklin is remembered as one of the major names connected to the lightning rod, bifocals, and the Franklin stove and practical science and everyday technology.
James Watt is widely associated with major improvements to the steam engine.
Watt's separate condenser made steam power far more efficient.

Alessandro Volta is widely associated with the electric battery.
Volta's voltaic pile is often described as the first true battery.

Samuel Morse is widely associated with the telegraph and Morse code.
Morse helped turn electrical signaling into a practical communication system.

Charles Babbage is widely associated with the Difference Engine and Analytical Engine concepts.
Babbage's designs anticipated many ideas that later appeared in modern computers.
He is often called a father of the computer even though his most ambitious machines were never fully built in his lifetime.
Histories of computing machinery almost always highlight Charles Babbage when discussing the Difference Engine and Analytical Engine concepts.
Charles Babbage's work helped move computing machinery forward in practical, everyday ways.

Charles Babbage is remembered as one of the major names connected to the Difference Engine and Analytical Engine concepts and computing machinery.
Ada Lovelace is widely associated with the first published algorithm intended for a machine.
Lovelace's notes on Babbage's Analytical Engine became foundational in computing history.
She imagined machines might one day create music and art as well as perform calculations.
Histories of early computer science almost always highlight Ada Lovelace when discussing the first published algorithm intended for a machine.
Ada Lovelace's work helped move early computer science forward in practical, everyday ways.

Alexander Graham Bell is widely associated with the telephone.
Bell's name is permanently linked with the history of voice communication over wires.

Thomas Edison is widely associated with the phonograph and practical electric-light systems.
Edison's Menlo Park laboratory became a symbol of organized industrial research.

Nikola Tesla is widely associated with the AC induction motor and alternating-current power systems.

The Wright brothers is widely associated with the first successful powered airplane.

Granville T. Woods is widely associated with improvements in railway telegraphy and electrical systems.

Stephanie Kwolek is widely associated with Kevlar.
Kwolek developed Kevlar while working at DuPont on strong but lightweight fibers.

Grace Hopper is widely associated with the first compiler and major programming-language innovations.
Hopper helped make computers easier to program by supporting the use of human-readable code.

Tim Berners-Lee is widely associated with the World Wide Web.
Berners-Lee created the web while working at CERN and also helped define core standards such as HTML and HTTP.

Douglas Engelbart is widely associated with the computer mouse and many ideas in interactive computing.
Engelbart's famous 1968 demonstration showcased the mouse, hypertext, collaborative editing, and video conferencing.

Archimedes is widely associated with the Archimedes screw and classic mechanical ideas.
